narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which evaluates applicants based on their likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, estimated a 57.6% chance that you would fail it. This prediction draws from your academic record, LSAT score, family background, and other details like planning to attend full-time. The two biggest factors pushing the model toward predicting failure were your Year 3 school decile ranking, which placed you in the bottom tenth of your peers, and your Year 1 decile, also in the lowest tenth—both well below the typical middle-of-the-pack averages for admitted students. These early and later undergraduate performance indicators stood out as the strongest signals of potential struggle on the demanding bar exam. On a more positive note, your LSAT score of 34 acted as a counterbalance, pulling the prediction toward success since it's a solid mark, though still under the average of about 37 seen in stronger profiles. Your undergraduate GPA of 3.10 was close to average, and your middle-income family background and full-time enrollment plans didn't sway things much either way, as they're common among applicants. As a Black male applicant, we recognize the unique challenges you may have faced in your academic journey, and while the model focuses purely on these predictors of bar success, your determination shines through in pursuing law despite those hurdles. Overall, your profile compared to typical students who pass the bar shows weaker early rankings that outweighed your other strengths, tipping the scales toward rejection to protect the program's outcomes. This isn't a judgment on your potential or character—many students improve dramatically with targeted effort. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score, strengthening your upper-year grades through additional coursework or tutoring, and reapplying next cycle with an updated profile that addresses these key areas. You could also seek mentorship from successful Black lawyers who navigated similar paths for personalized advice. With focused steps, you can shift this prediction in your favor and pursue your legal ambitions. Keep pushing—you've got this.
